summary:
Claim: Black deposits on the conjunctiva in glaucoma patients are most commonly associated with prostaglandin analogs.  
Evidence: - Long-term use of prostaglandin analogs (e.g., latanoprost) causes pigment shedding from the ciliary body due to enhanced uveoscleral outflow. - Clinical data show significantly higher incidence of conjunctival pigmentation in patients on prostaglandin therapy versus other glaucoma medications (e.g., beta-blockers, carbonic anhydrase inhibitors).  
Answer: A: Prostaglandins

Answer: A: Prostaglandins. Answer choice: A
